info.title: Desearch API
info.description: The Desearch API is a powerful suite of AI-driven tools designed to integrate natural language processing, AI-powered search, image generation, and data extraction into your applications. Whether you’re building intelligent search engines, automating content creation, analyzing text data, or generating AI-based media, Desearch provides a robust and scalable solution.

post.description: The Desearch API allows you to perform AI-powered web searches, gathering relevant information from multiple sources, including web pages, research papers, and social media discussions.

200.description: A JSON object mapping tool names to their search results.

schema.title: Response Desearch Desearch Ai Search Post

post.description: This API allows users to search for links related to a given query (prompt) using multiple tools, excluding X (Twitter) Search. The API returns a list of relevant sources from selected platforms such as web pages, YouTube, Wikipedia, and ArXiv.

200.description: A JSON object mapping tool names to their search results.

post.description: The Х Posts Search API allows users to search for relevant links based on Х search queries with leveraging AI-powered models. This API analyze links from Х posts that match the given prompt. This API is useful for tracking trends, gathering insights, and retrieving real-time information from Х.

200.description: A JSON object mapping Х Search to its search results.

get.description: The X Search API enables users to retrieve relevant links and tweets based on specified search queries without utilizing AI-driven models. It analyzes links from X posts that align with the provided search criteria.

parameters.name: blue_verified

schema.description: Filter for blue checkmark verified users
schema.title: Blue Verified

parameters.description: Filter for blue checkmark verified users

200.description: A JSON object mapping X Search to its search results.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information for multiple posts by providing their respective URLs. This endpoint extracts metadata, content, and relevant engagement metrics associated with each specified URL.

parameters.name: urls

schema.description: List of urls that is to be retrieved.
schema.title: Urls

parameters.description: List of urls that is to be retrieved.

200.description: A JSON object mapping tweet URLs to their details.

schema.title: Response Twitter By Urls Twitter Urls Get

get.description: Retrieve comprehensive details of a post by specifying its unique ID. This endpoint provides metadata, content, and engagement metrics associated with the specified post.

200.description: A JSON object representing the post details.

get.description: The X posts search by user API allows users to search for relevant links or tweets based on X search queries without leveraging AI-powered models. This API analyze links from X posts that match the given prompt.

200.description: A JSON object mapping Twitter Search to its search results.

schema.title: Response Tweets By User Twitter Post User Get

get.description: Retrieve the list of users who retweeted a specific post by its ID. Supports pagination via cursor.

parameters.name: id

schema.description: The ID of the post to get retweeters for
schema.title: Id

parameters.description: The ID of the post to get retweeters for

200.description: A JSON object containing the list of users who retweeted the post.

get.description: Retrieve user's timeline posts by specifying their username. This endpoint fetches the latest tweets posted by the user associated with the provided username.

200.description: A JSON object mapping X Posts

get.description: The tweets and replies by user API allows users to search for relevant links or tweets based on X search queries without leveraging AI-powered models. This API analyze links from X posts that match the given prompt.

parameters.description: The number of tweets to fetch.

get.description: The Tweets and Replies by User API enables users to retrieve relevant tweets and replies based on specified search queries without relying on AI-powered models. It analyzes and extracts links from X posts that align with the provided search criteria.

schema.description: The ID of the post to search for.

get.description: Retrieve trending topics on X for a given location using its WOEID (Where On Earth ID).

parameters.name: woeid

schema.description: The WOEID of the location (e.g. 23424977 for United States). WOEID List: https://gist.github.com/tedyblood/5bb5a9f78314cc1f478b3dd7cde790b9
schema.title: Woeid

parameters.description: The WOEID of the location (e.g. 23424977 for United States). WOEID List: https://gist.github.com/tedyblood/5bb5a9f78314cc1f478b3dd7cde790b9

parameters.name: count

schema.description: The number of trends to return. Minimum is 30, maximum is 100.
schema.title: Count

parameters.description: The number of trends to return. Minimum is 30, maximum is 100.

200.description: A JSON object containing the list of trending topics.

get.description: This API allows users to search for any information over the web. This replicates a typical search engine experience, where users can search for any information they need.

parameters.name: query

schema.description: The search query string, e.g., 'latest news on AI'.
schema.title: Query

parameters.description: The search query string, e.g., 'latest news on AI'.

parameters.name: start

schema.description: How many results to skip. It's used for pagination. (e.g., 0 (default) is the first page of results, 10 is the 2nd page of results, 20 is the 3rd page of results, etc.).
schema.title: Start

parameters.description: How many results to skip. It's used for pagination. (e.g., 0 (default) is the first page of results, 10 is the 2nd page of results, 20 is the 3rd page of results, etc.).

201.description: A JSON object mapping Web Search to its search results.

get.description: The Desearch API allows you to crawl web content

parameters.name: format

schema.description: Format of the content to be returned. Options are 'html' or 'text'. Default is 'html'.
schema.title: Format

parameters.description: Format of the content to be returned. Options are 'html' or 'text'. Default is 'html'.

post.description: Submit a forecasting question to the Numinous AI superforecasting system powered by Bittensor Subnet 6. Returns a prediction job ID to poll for results. Supports two modes: **structured** (title + description + cutoff) for precise results, or **query** (natural language question) for quick exploration.

202.description: A prediction job ID and initial PENDING status.

get.description: Poll the status of a prediction job created via POST /numinous/forecasts. Poll every 5 seconds — jobs typically complete within 30–120 seconds. The `prediction_id` UUID acts as an access token; no API key is required for polling.

200.description: Current job status and result when completed.

example.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.example: What are the recent sport events?

count.title: Count
count.description: The number of results to return per source. Min 10. Max 200.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.example: What are the recent sport events?

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: List of tools to search with

count.title: Count
count.description: The number of results to return per source. Min 10. Max 200.

prediction.title: Prediction
prediction.description: Probability estimate in [0.0, 1.0]. E.g. 0.72 means 72% probability of YES.

forecaster_name.title: Forecaster Name
forecaster_name.description: Identifier of the forecaster that produced the result.

forecasted_at.title: Forecasted At
forecasted_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the forecast was generated.

metadata.description: Forecaster metadata including miner info and optional reasoning.

parsed_fields.description: Fields extracted from the natural language query. Present only in query mode.

miner_uid.title: Miner Uid
miner_uid.description: Miner UID on the Bittensor subnet.

event_title.title: Event Title
event_title.description: Event title used for the forecast.

event_cutoff.title: Event Cutoff
event_cutoff.description: ISO 8601 cutoff datetime for the event.

prediction_id.title: Prediction Id
prediction_id.description: Unique identifier for the prediction job. Use this to poll for results.

status.description: Initial job status. Always PENDING on creation.
Current job status: PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, or FAILED.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the job was created.

result.description: Forecast result. Present when status is COMPLETED.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error description. Present when status is FAILED.

example.text:
Bittensor $TAO seems oversold here.

Growth up but price is down

Price Valuation (Next 12 Months)

Factors  

Technical: Trading above 200-day MA; support at $350-$380.  

Fundamental: Halving, subnet growth, institutional interest (e.g., Grayscale’s Bittensor Trust).  

Sentiment: Strong staking (72%) and developer activity.

Forecast (Jun 2025-Jun 2026)  

Conservative: $350-$600  

Base: $600-$900  

Bullish: $900-$1,200

system_message.title: System Message

system_message.example: Summarize the content by categorizing key points into 'Pros' and 'Cons' sections.

scoring_system_message.title: Scoring System Message
scoring_system_message.description: System message for scoring the response
scoring_system_message.example:
Business Relevance Scoring Guide:

                    Task: As an evaluator, determine how well a tweet represents a business opportunity for the agent based on contextual relevance to the specific agent use case provided.

                    IMPORTANT: Only score highly for needs that directly relate to the agent's specific use case. Similar but different problems should receive lower scores unless they specifically mention the agent's domain.

                    Agent use case: Find people who needs SERP API service and is looking for cheaper options for scraping

                    Scoring Criteria:

                    Score 2 - No Business Opportunity:
                    - Criteria: Tweet is completely unrelated to the agent's use case or shows no indication of need for the agent's specific offering
                    - Context: Author is not expressing any pain point, question, or situation relevant to the agent's domain
                    - Examples:
                    - Agent Use Case: "Find people needing SERP API services"
                    - Tweet: "Just had amazing pizza for lunch!" → Score 2 (completely unrelated)
                    - Tweet: "Our streaming API is having problems" → Score 2 (different API domain)

                    Score 5 - Potential Interest:
                    - Criteria: Tweet shows indirect relevance to the agent's domain but lacks clear intent, urgency, or specific need
                    - Context: Author mentions related topics but doesn't express explicit need for the agent's specific solution
                    - Examples:
                    - Agent Use Case: "Find people needing SERP API services"
                    - Tweet: "Working on a new web scraping project" → Score 5 (related activity, no explicit SERP need)
                    - Tweet: "APIs are getting expensive these days" → Score 5 (general API concern, not SERP-specific)

                    Score 9 - Strong Business Opportunity:
                    - Criteria: Tweet indicates a clear need, problem, or interest that directly aligns with the agent's specific use case
                    - Context: Author is seeking solutions, expressing frustration, asking for recommendations, or describing challenges specifically in the agent's domain
                    - Examples:
                    - Agent Use Case: "Find people needing SERP API services"
                    - Tweet: "Anyone know a reliable API for Google search results? Current one keeps failing" → Score 9 (direct SERP API need)
                    - Tweet: "SERP API costs are killing our budget, need alternatives" → Score 9 (specific SERP API problem)
                    
                    Output Format:
                    Score: [2, 5, or 9], Explanation: [Brief explanation focusing on how specifically this relates to the agent's use case and the level of expressed need]
